 {"id":"gt-ebl","title":"CLI: names commands for polecat naming pool","description":"Polecat naming pool for auto-generated names.

## Commands

### gt names generate
```
gt names generate [--count N]
```
Generate N names from pool.

### gt names add
```
gt names add <name>...
```
Add custom names to pool.

### gt names list
```
gt names list
```
Show available and used names.

### gt names reset
```
gt names reset [--keep-used]
```
Reset pool to defaults.

## Config File
<rig>/town/naming.json:
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "auto_refill": true,
  "refill_threshold": 5,
  "pool": {
    "available": ["Toast", "Nux", "Capable", ...],
    "used": ["Alice", "Bob"]
  }
}
```

## Default Pool
Mad Max themed: Toast, Nux, Capable, Furiosa, Immortan, etc.

## Integration
gt polecat add calls naming pool if no name given:
```go
if name == "" {
    name, err = naming.Generate(rigPath)
}
```

## New Package
internal/naming/
├── pool.go     # Pool management
└── defaults.go # Default name lists

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-generate names on polecat add
- [ ] Track used vs available
- [ ] Auto-refill when low
- [ ] Custom names addable","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:33.592129-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:13.882465-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-lek6","title":"gt rig reset --stale: Clear orphaned in_progress items","description":"Reset in_progress issues when their assigned agent no longer exists.

## Problem
When polecats die without cleanup, their issues remain in_progress forever.
Need a way to bulk-reset these orphaned items.

## Command
```bash
gt rig reset --stale [--dry-run]
```

## Logic
For each in_progress issue in rig:
1. Parse assignee (e.g., "gastown/furiosa")
2. Map to tmux session name (gt-gastown-furiosa)
3. If session does NOT exist:
   - Reset status to "open"
   - Clear assignee
4. Exception: skip crew/* assignees (persistent identities)
   OR check if crew tmux session exists

## Output
```
Resetting stale work in gastown:
  gt-abc: gastown/furiosa (no session) → open
  gt-def: gastown/nux (no session) → open
  Skipped: gt-xyz: gastown/crew/max (persistent)
Reset 2 issues, skipped 1
```

## Related
- gt-2kz: CLI cleanup commands for stale state
- gt-rdmw: orphan-check in deacon patrol
- gt-orphans command (list orphaned molecules)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:46.962413-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented --stale flag for gt rig reset with dry-run support"}

 {"id":"gt-lnji","title":"gt polecat git-state command for pre-kill verification","description":"Add git-state subcommand to gt polecat for Witness pre-kill verification.

## Usage
```bash
gt polecat git-state <rig>/<polecat>
```

## Output
```
Git State: gastown/furiosa

  Working Tree: clean | dirty
  Uncommitted:  0 files | N files (list)
  Unpushed:     0 commits | N commits ahead
  Stashes:      0 | N stashes

  Verdict: CLEAN (safe to kill) | DIRTY (needs cleanup)
```

## JSON output (--json flag)
```json
{
  "clean": true,
  "uncommitted_files": [],
  "unpushed_commits": 0,
  "stash_count": 0
}
```

## Used by
- Witness pre-kill verification (mol-witness-patrol)
- Manual cleanup checks

## Implementation
- Check git status in polecat worktree
- Check git log origin/main..HEAD
- Check git stash list","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:10.035052-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:35:06.34384-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:35:06.34384-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt polecat git-state command for pre-kill verification"}
 {"id":"gt-lno","title":"Swarm state persistence: manifest + state + events","description":"Upgrade swarm persistence to match PGT pattern.

## Current State
Single .gastown/swarms.json with all swarms.

## Target State (per-swarm)
```
.gastown/swarms/
└── <swarm-id>/
    ├── manifest.json  # Immutable config
    ├── state.json     # Mutable progress
    ├── events.jsonl   # Audit log
    └── report.md      # Generated report
```

## File Schemas

### manifest.json (immutable after creation)
```json
{
  "id": "sw-1",
  "title": "Epic description",
  "epic_id": "gt-abc",
  "rig": "gastown",
  "base_commit": "abc123",
  "integration_branch": "swarm/sw-1",
  "target_branch": "main",
  "workers": ["Toast", "Nux"],
  "tasks": [{"id": "gt-xyz", "title": "..."}],
  "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}
```

### state.json (mutable)
```json
{
  "state": "active",
  "task_states": {
    "gt-xyz": {"state": "merged", "assignee": "Toast"}
  },
  "updated_at": "2024-01-01T01:00:00Z",
  "error": null
}
```

### events.jsonl (append-only audit)
```jsonl
{"event": "created", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_assigned", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_merged", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
```

## Benefits
- Audit trail via events.jsonl
- Manifest immutability prevents corruption
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Per-swarm isolation

## Migration
Keep backward compat with swarms.json during transition.

## Files to Modify
- internal/swarm/manager.go: Refactor persistence
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore → directory-based

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-swarm directory structure
- [ ] Events logged to JSONL
- [ ] Manifest immutable after creation
- [ ] List command aggregates from directories","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:14.151538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-tr3d","title":"Zombie session detection for refinery/witness","description":"A Claude session can crash or hang while still appearing as 'running' in tmux. The current IsRunning checks only verify tmux session exists, not that Claude is actually processing.

## Problem
- Refinery session might be hung (Claude crashed, infinite loop, etc.)
- tmux HasSession returns true, so auto-start doesn't trigger
- MRs pile up with no processing

## Solution Options
1. **Activity-based detection**: Check last output timestamp in tmux
2. **Heartbeat file**: Agents write timestamp to file, check staleness
3. **Prompt detection**: Look for Claude '>' prompt indicating idle/stuck

## Related
- gt-bjft (spawn auto-start) assumes IsRunning means healthy
- Witness patrol assumes running refinery is processing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:02:51.089112-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:02:51.089112-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-tr3d","depends_on_id":"gt-bjft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:12.10595-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-tubn","title":"Track operational stats as entity/audit beads","description":"Witness and refinery track operational stats in JSON files:
- total_merged, today_merged, total_failed (refinery)
- total_checks, total_nudges, total_escalations (witness)

For HOP entity CV tracking, these could become:
- type=audit beads (daily roll-ups of operational metrics)
- Entity chain entries showing validator activity

This provides observability and contributes to entity reputation tracking (e.g., refinery X has merged 500 PRs with 2% failure rate).

Not critical for launch but aligns with HOP Platform of Platforms vision.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:07:30.404073-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T22:07:30.404073-08:00"}
